rules.description: Report and evidence screens must identify the current project/ad hoc context, selected/latest run or report, generated timestamp or status, and relevant counts when users are inspecting audit output.

examples.good: Run Timeline shows Project: Workspace Control, Selected run: Latest report, generated timestamp, status, and phase list.
examples.bad: Run Timeline shows phases but does not say which run/report, project, timestamp, or status is being reviewed.

rules.description: Raw JSON, debug payload, exact report data, and JSON inspection screens must expose a visible copy/export/download action.
expectedEvidence: visible Copy JSON, Copy raw payload, Copy report JSON, Download JSON, or Export JSON control

examples.good: Raw JSON shows a formatted report payload and a Copy JSON button.
examples.bad: Raw JSON shows a formatted payload with no Copy JSON, Download JSON, or Export JSON action.

rules.description: Screenshot galleries and modals must show state/scenario/action/URL context near screenshots so images are usable as QA evidence.

examples.good: Each screenshot thumbnail lists Scenario: navigation smoke test, Step: click Issues, URL: /#issues.
examples.bad: Screenshots page only lists state-1.png and graph-explorer.png with no scenario, step, URL, or action context.

rules.description: Graph views must be interpretable through a visible mode/scope, legend, filter/search affordance, and selected-node detail or equivalent explanation.

examples.good: Graph Explorer shows mode Crawl Graph, a legend, issue filters, and selected node details.
examples.bad: Graph Explorer shows many nodes and edges with no legend, filters, or selected-node detail.

rules.description: Issues empty states must explain what was checked and what to do next, rather than merely saying there are no issues.

examples.good: No issues found after checking 11 scenarios, console/network events, and screenshots. Run another audit or inspect Scenarios.
examples.bad: Issues page only says No issues.

rules.description: Fix packet screens must show the issue, suspected files, prompt, verification command, pass conditions, copy action, and repair status.

examples.good: Fix packet detail has Copy prompt, suspected files, verification command, and manual/Codex status.
examples.bad: A fix packet title is shown without prompt, files, verification command, or copy action.

rules.description: Repeated row actions in history/list UIs need unique accessible names or scoped locator guidance so users and tests can distinguish them.

examples.good: Each row action says Reopen plan run: Add OwnersPage, May 7 1:45 PM.
examples.bad: Ten plan-run cards all contain a button named Reopen.

rules.description: Status and chip text should not be duplicated or read twice in cards/lists because it reduces scanability and can confuse assistive technology.

examples.good: A plan-run card says completed and Semantic Off once each.
examples.bad: A plan-run card reads completed completed Semantic Off Semantic Off.

rules.description: Summary pages should lead with human-readable status, counts, top issues, screenshots, and next actions; raw JSON belongs in an explicit advanced/debug view.

examples.good: Summary shows overall status, scenarios passed, issue count, fix packets, screenshots, and next actions.
examples.bad: Summary primarily displays a raw JSON blob with no human-readable report overview.

rules.description: A navigation label must lead to content that visibly matches the promised screen or workflow.

examples.good: Clicking Screenshots opens a page headed Screenshots with screenshot evidence.
examples.bad: Clicking Run Timeline opens a generic raw report page that does not mention timeline phases.
